title: BREAKING: Savannah Guthrie in new video: 'We believe our mom is still out there - we need your help'
autoCaptionsText: All right, everybody. We have breaking news. We have just received a brand new video from Savannah Guthrie as we are a little over an hour away from one of the deadlines in the letter for this ransom payment. We're getting it ready. We are about to play it for you in just a moment, but I can tell you that in it, she says, "We need your help." And we're going to play this for you as soon as we get it turned around. Just stick with us here. chief correspondent Jonathan Hunt on the ground in Tucson as we wait to show that to you in just a moment. Jonathan, >> and interestingly, Martha, this new message is not addressed to the kidnapper or kidnappers. It is addressed to the general public. Uh in it, Savannah Guthrie thanks everybody for their prayers. She says, "We are in an hour of desperation." and she said that we the Guthrie siblings believe our mother is still out there. Uh she hears your prayers, Savannah says and she says we all need your help. She is out there somewhere. Uh but it certainly sounds from the tone of that message which our viewers will hear shortly. uh Martha that the investigation is at something of a dead end at this point because Savannah Guthri's tone in this is absolutely uh desperate saying that law enforcement are working as hard as they can but they need the public's help and she appeals to anybody anywhere well outside of Tucson well outside of Arizona if you see something that you think might be helpful in any way shape or form please contact law enforcement. So, this uh I think it is fair to characterize as a message of utter desperation. Once again, a gut-wrenching thing uh to listen to as all of Savannah Guthri's videos have been. Martha >> Nathan, um you know, so quick question here and and you make a number of good points. She's just addressing people generally. Um and we're going to play it in just a second. >> Um but there's no mention in this video like the last one, Jonathan, we will pay. We understand. Uh so what do you what might this signal? I mean, I know you don't know the answer to this. We're just, you know, sort of thinking it through. Yeah. About this pending ransom time and and whether or not perhaps they do not >> put credence in that anymore potentially. >> Yeah, it's a perfectly fair question, Martha. This is a this is a very different message. The ones we have seen so far, three videos uh from the family, they have all been a direct uh message to the kidnapper. This now uh is moving beyond that. This is a message to the public saying we need your help. It almost feels and we're all speculating here, Martha. Of course, we're all trying to read a tone, but it almost feels like uh they feel as though this has taken a step backwards rather than forward. You're very you're absolutely accurate in picking up on that tone. >> Um, thank you so much, Jonathan Hunt. Let's play this video. Stick around for a second. Let's watch this. >> Hi there, everybody. Um, I wanted to come on and just share a few thoughts as we enter into another week of this nightmare. Um, I just want to say first of all, thank you so much for all of the prayers and the love that we have felt, my sister and brother and I, and that our mom has felt because we believe that somehow someway she is feeling these prayers and that God is lifting her even in this moment and in this darkest place. We believe our mom is still out there. We need your help. Law enforcement is working tirelessly around the clock trying to bring her home, trying to find her. She was taken and we don't know where and we need your help. So, I'm coming on just to ask you not just for your prayers, but no matter where you are, even if you're far from Tucson, if you see anything, if you hear anything, if there's anything at all that seems strange to you that you report to law enforcement, we are at an hour of desperation and we need your help. >> Hey, Sean Hannity here. Hey, click here to subscribe to Fox News YouTube page and catch our hottest interviews and most compelling analysis. You will not get it anywhere else.
